title: Reading Time Calculator
shortDescription: Estimate how long any text will take to read based on word count.
metaDescription: Estimate reading time from word count and see how many minutes a text will take to finish.
intro: Enter your text or word count to get an estimated reading time in minutes and seconds.
overview:
Use this calculator to estimate how long a text will take to read before you publish, send, or review it. It turns word count into a clear time estimate so you can judge whether a post feels too short, too long, or just right.

The result is based on an average reading pace, so it gives a practical estimate rather than an exact stopwatch value. That makes it useful for article planning, newsletter drafting, lesson prep, and checking whether a document fits a target reading length.
howItWorks: Paste your text or enter the word count.
The tool calculates reading time using a standard reading pace.
Review the minutes and seconds, then compare against your target length.

useCases.title: Blog post planning
useCases.description: Check whether a draft reads like a 3-minute post, a 5-minute article, or something longer before you publish.

useCases.title: Newsletter length check
useCases.description: Estimate how much time subscribers will spend on a weekly update and adjust the copy if it feels too dense.

useCases.title: Course lesson notes
useCases.description: Work out whether a lesson handout is short enough for a quick read or needs trimming for classroom use.

useCases.title: Client review documents
useCases.description: Set expectations for reports, proposals, or summaries by showing the approximate reading time up front.

examples.title: Short article
examples.description: A concise post, email, or note with light reading time.

examples.title: Medium blog draft
examples.description: A typical article length that reads in a few minutes.

examples.title: Long feature draft
examples.description: A detailed piece that requires more time and attention.

faq.question: What reading speed does the calculator use?
faq.answer: It uses a standard average reading pace to convert words into an estimated time. The exact value can vary depending on how dense or technical the text is.

faq.question: Why might two texts with the same word count take different times to read?
faq.answer: Headings, long sentences, technical terms, lists, and code can slow reading down even when the word count is the same.

faq.question: Should I count titles, captions, and bullet points?
faq.answer: If they are part of the reading experience, include them. Leaving them out can make the estimate shorter than the real reading time.

faq.question: Is this the same as speaking time?
faq.answer: No. Reading time is usually different from presentation or narration time because reading pace is not the same as speaking pace.
